task:
BUILD ATOM 1 (oz-autonomy, ADR-0040): the code-level `oz-action` scope guard, test-proven. The governance gate is cleared — ADR-0040 is founder-Accepted and carry-forward pointers + the decisions index already point to it; build atoms are now allowed. This atom owns the ADR-0040 deferred 'owner-map-first atom' and satisfies the priority's third 'Verified when' bullet (a code-level scope guard proven by test). Do NOT wire the daemon `oz-action` tool/handler or the conversational author round yet — those are later atoms. Reuse the existing spine; do not invent a second partition or commit path.

FIRST, write a short owner-map (as a top-of-file comment on the new module, ~6-10 lines) naming: the source of truth for the allow-list (this new constant), every surface that will consume it (the future daemon oz-action handler), and the tests that pin it. Then implement.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a single source-of-truth constant `OZ_ACTION_SCOPE` (the ADR-0040 §1 reversible-edit allow-list) and a matching held-back assertion. Place it in `packages/core/src/` (a small new module under `commit-gate/` or `write-scope/`, exported from `packages/core/src/index.ts`) so both core and daemon can import the ONE owner. The allow-list is EXACTLY ADR-0040 §1: `cocoder/priorities/order.json`, `cocoder/tickets/**`, `docs/**`, governed top-level `*.md` doc surfaces, and edits to existing `cocoder/priorities/*.md` bodies. It must NOT include `cocoder/decisions/**` Objective creation, product code, secrets, or install-local state.
2. The lane reuses `commitScoped(..., {commitOnlyScope: true})` (ADR-0040 §2: only allowed paths land, out-of-lane held back and surfaced). Do not add new commit logic — just the scope constant the lane will pass.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (exit = the named tests pass):
- A new core test (e.g. `packages/core/tests/oz-action-scope.test.ts`) proves, via `partitionByScope`/`commitScoped` with `OZ_ACTION_SCOPE` and `commitOnlyScope:true`: (a) IN-LANE paths land — `cocoder/priorities/order.json`, `cocoder/tickets/open/0099-x.md`, `docs/foo.md`, and an existing-priority body edit `cocoder/priorities/oz-autonomy.md`; (b) HARD-EXCLUSION paths are held back as `outOfLane`, NEVER committed — `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` (product code `packages/*/src/`), a secret file, and an install-local state path (e.g. `local/runs/run_212/run.json`). Assert committedFiles excludes every §4 path and outOfLane includes them.
- Add/confirm a test that the existing `author` validator refuses a net-new priority Objective when the founder-approval field is absent (ADR-0025 §4 / ADR-0040 §3) — extend `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts` if not already covered; do NOT weaken that refusal into a tautology.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: all existing core + daemon suites, especially `packages/core/tests/commit-gate.test.ts`, `plays-request.test.ts`, and `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts` / `oz-chat.test.ts`. Do not change `commitScoped`/`gateCommitRepair`/`partitionByScope` behavior — only add the new constant + tests.

WRITE-SCOPE: `packages/core/src/**`, `packages/core/tests/**`, `packages/daemon/tests/**`. Do not touch `packages/daemon/src/**` in this atom (handler wiring is atom 2). Do not run git.

EXIT CRITERION (run and report exit codes): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test -- authoring-play` both green, and report the new oz-action-scope test file's pass count.

BUILD ATOM 2 (oz-autonomy, ADR-0040 §2): the daemon `oz-action` self-direct write lane. This satisfies the priority's SECOND 'Verified when' bullet — Oz performs a reversible governance edit and the daemon gate-commits it as an `oz-action` commit whose receipt shows only allowed paths landed and any out-of-scope path held back. REUSE the existing spine — do NOT add a second commit path, and do NOT restate the scope globs (import the single owner `OZ_ACTION_SCOPE` from `@cocoder/core`, landed in atom 1). Mirror the existing `requestOzRepair` / `author` lane shape exactly; this is the same capability pattern as ADR-0025's authoring lane.

WHAT TO CHANGE (all in `packages/daemon/`):
1. `src/launcher.ts`: add exported `requestOzAction(ctx, {workspaceId, instruction})` mirroring `requestOzRepair`: same in-flight guard (refuse 409 if a run is in flight), validate non-empty instruction (<=4000 chars), resolve the Oz CLI/model via `resolveOzRepairTarget`, write a turn log under `local/oz/<workspaceId>/oz-action-*.log`, build a concise prompt (a new `buildOzActionPrompt`) that tells Oz to make ONLY a reversible edit to existing governance (reorder `cocoder/priorities/order.json`, open/close `cocoder/tickets/**`, a narrow `docs/**` or top-level `*.md` doc fix, or a non-Objective edit to an existing `cocoder/priorities/*.md`), to NOT touch product code / secrets / install-local state / net-new Objectives / lifecycle, and to NOT run git. Then call `runHeadlessThenGateCommit` with `scope: OZ_ACTION_SCOPE`, `commitOnlyScope: true`, `commitMessage: 'oz-action'`, `author: {name:'oz-action', email:'oz-action@cocoder.local'}`, `auditAction: 'oz-action'`, `eventType: 'oz-action'`, and the pre/exit error builders. Do NOT modify `requestOzRepair`, `requestAuthoringPlay`, or `runHeadlessThenGateCommit` behavior — additive only.
2. `src/oz-host.ts`: add `'oz-action'` to `ToolName`, document it in the tools-list prompt (one line: reversible governance edit, gate-committed, out-of-lane held back), and wire its OZ_TOOL parse/validate (requires a non-empty `instruction` string, mirror the `requiredString` pattern used for other verbs).
3. `src/oz-chat.ts`: add the `OzCommand` kind `{kind:'oz-action', instruction:string}`, register the op (`requestOzAction`) in the ops object + its type, add the dispatch case calling it, and an `ozActionReply` renderer mirroring `repairReply` (report committed paths + sha, and surface held-back out-of-lane paths as 'held back, NOT committed').

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (exit = named tests pass):
- New `tests/oz-action.test.ts` (mirror `tests/oz-repair.test.ts`, stubbing the headless turn + git) proves: (a) an in-lane edit (e.g. a changed `cocoder/tickets/open/0099-x.md` + `cocoder/priorities/order.json`) gate-commits as `oz-action` with those paths in committedPaths and a commit message `oz-action`; (b) an out-of-lane changed path (e.g. `packages/daemon/src/foo.ts` or `local/runs/x/run.json`) is in `outOfLanePaths`, NEVER in committedPaths; (c) in-flight refusal returns 409 and commits nothing.
- A tool-round test (extend `tests/oz-chat.test.ts` and/or `tests/oz-agent-chat.test.ts`): an `OZ_TOOL {"tool":"oz-action","args":{"instruction":"..."}}` line dispatches `requestOzAction` and renders the receipt reply; a missing/empty `instruction` is rejected without executing.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: existing `oz-repair.test.ts`, `authoring-play.test.ts`, `oz-chat.test.ts`, `oz-agent-chat.test.ts`, `mutations.test.ts`. NOTE: the full daemon suite has a KNOWN pre-existing ordering flake in `mutations.test.ts` (a ticket-close/index assertion) that PASSES when that file is run in isolation — do not chase it; confirm it still passes alone (`vitest run mutations`).

WRITE-SCOPE: `packages/daemon/src/**`, `packages/daemon/tests/**` only. Do not touch `packages/core/**` (atom 1 owns the scope constant). Do not run git.

EXIT CRITERION (run and report exit codes + pass counts): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run oz-action oz-chat oz-agent-chat oz-repair` all green, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run mutations` green in isolation, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` green.

BUILD ATOM 3 (oz-autonomy, ADR-0040 §3/§4): the CODE-LEVEL founder-Objective guard on the authoring dispatch. This satisfies the second half of the priority's THIRD 'Verified when' bullet — Oz cannot commit a net-new priority Objective without the founder-supplied Objective present. CONTEXT: today the create-priority Play enforces 'a real ## Objective section' only at the PROMPT level (Play steps 1 & 7 instruct the agent); there is no code-level backstop, so a misbehaving headless turn could commit a priority with a missing/empty Objective. ADR-0025 deliberately added NO separate boolean approval field ('no second approval policy') — the founder approval IS the Objective arriving in the invocation. So the guard = the committed priority file must have a real, non-empty Objective; refuse the commit otherwise. The conversational author-commit path itself (bullet 1) already works via the existing `author` tool — do NOT rebuild it; it is proven end-to-end by the next atom's proof harness.

WHAT TO CHANGE (in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts`):
1. In the `requestAuthoringPlay` dispatch, add a code-level pre-commit validation that runs ONLY for `create-priority` and `edit-priority` (NOT archive-priority): after the headless turn succeeds and AFTER `registerLivePriorities`, but BEFORE the gate-commit, inspect the priority files the turn changed under `cocoder/priorities/*.md` (use `ctx.git.changedFiles(workspace.path)` filtered to that glob) and, for each, load it via core `loadPriority(prioritiesDir, id)` (already exported from `@cocoder/core`) and REFUSE if `objective === null` (core's `parseObjective` already returns null for a missing OR empty `## Objective` section — reuse it, do NOT write a second objective parser).
2. On refusal: commit NOTHING and return a clear failure LaunchResult (e.g. status 422, `ok:false`, `error: 'refusing to commit priority "<id>": missing founder-approved Objective'`, empty committedPaths, null commitSha) surfaced through the existing body shape so `authoringReply` shows it. Do not throw a vague 'registration step failed' — give the dedicated message. Reuse the existing no-commit/audit/event path so behavior matches the other refusals.

ELEGANCE/ONE-OWNER: the Objective check MUST reuse core `loadPriority(...).objective` (the same parser the Play step 7 references). Do not duplicate the `## Objective` regex or invent a new approval field.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (exit = named tests pass), add to `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts`:
- create-priority whose headless turn writes `cocoder/priorities/<id>.md` with NO `## Objective` section (or an empty one) is REFUSED: nothing is committed (HEAD unchanged / committedPaths empty), and the error names the missing Objective.
- SYMMETRIC (defect-class): edit-priority whose turn blanks an existing priority's Objective is likewise refused with nothing committed.
- archive-priority is EXEMPT (its existing happy path still commits).
- The existing create-priority happy path (Objective 'Ship alpha.') still commits exactly the one file — must stay green.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: all existing `authoring-play.test.ts`, `oz-chat.test.ts`, `oz-agent-chat.test.ts`, `oz-action.test.ts`, `oz-repair.test.ts`. NOTE the known pre-existing `mutations.test.ts` full-suite ordering flake that PASSES in isolation — do not chase it.

WRITE-SCOPE: `packages/daemon/src/**`, `packages/daemon/tests/**` only. Do not touch `packages/core/**` (reuse exported `loadPriority`). Do not run git.

EXIT CRITERION (report exit codes + pass counts): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run authoring-play oz-chat oz-agent-chat oz-action oz-repair` green, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon exec vitest run mutations` green in isolation, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` green.

BUILD ATOM 4 (oz-autonomy, final): a runnable proof harness `scripts/proof-oz-autonomy.mjs` that turns the priority's 'Verified when' into ONE command with a PASS/FAIL table and a real exit code (F18 — verification must be runnable, not a doc checklist). FOLLOW THE EXACT STRUCTURE of the existing `scripts/proof-governance-authoring.mjs`: run the REAL daemon/core Vitest suites via `--reporter=json --outputFile=`, map each clause to EXISTING named test titles, add filesystem checks, run the repo `pnpm typecheck` build gate LAST, print a `clause | verdict | evidence` table, and set `process.exitCode` (0 only if all required clauses PASS). Do NOT reimplement orchestration logic and do NOT author any new behavior tests — atoms 1-3 already proved the behavior; this harness only CITES and RUNS the existing tests. If a cited test title does not exist, the harness row must show MISSING and exit 1 (the proof-governance-authoring evaluateTests pattern already does this) — so cite the exact titles you find in the test files.

CLAUSES (map each to existing named tests unless noted; find the exact titles by reading the test files):
- A (Verified-when bullet 1 — Oz creates+commits a priority conversationally via the `author` spine with NO adhoc run): cite the author-tool + authoring-spine tests in `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts`, `oz-chat.test.ts`, `oz-agent-chat.test.ts` that prove create-priority commits through the spine via the one author tool action and immediate launch needs no manual commit (e.g. 'dispatches create-priority and commits the priority file through the repair spine', the author-tool one-action test in oz-agent-chat, and 'agent authoring commits a priority and immediate launch succeeds with no manual commit').
- B (bullet 2 — oz-action gate-commits a reversible edit; allowed paths land, out-of-lane held back): cite `packages/daemon/tests/oz-action.test.ts` ('gate-commits only reversible governance paths as oz-action and holds out-of-lane edits back', 'refuses while any run is in flight before spawning or committing') PLUS the oz-action tool-round test(s) you added to oz-chat.test.ts/oz-agent-chat.test.ts.
- C (bullet 3a — code-level scope guard: cannot write product code/secrets/install-local): cite `packages/core/tests/oz-action-scope.test.ts` ('matches only ADR-0040 reversible-edit paths', 'commitOnlyScope lands in-lane paths and holds hard exclusions out of lane').
- D (bullet 3b — cannot commit a net-new/blanked priority Objective): cite the three guard tests in `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts` ('refuses create-priority when the written priority has no founder-approved Objective', 'refuses edit-priority when the edit blanks an existing Objective', 'archive-priority is exempt from the Objective guard and still commits').
- E (bullet 4 — ADR-0040 founder-approved + carry-forward pointers): FILESYSTEM checks (not a test): assert `cocoder/decisions/0040-oz-write-side-autonomy.md` exists and contains 'Status:' with 'Accepted'; assert each of `cocoder/decisions/0016-deb-scoped-repair-fallback.md`, `0017-oz-orchestration-persona.md`, `0025-atomic-authoring-plays.md` contains a pointer to '0040'; assert the decisions index `cocoder/decisions/README.md` references '0040'.

EXIT CRITERION (run it and paste the full table + exit code): `node scripts/proof-oz-autonomy.mjs` prints an all-green required table and exits 0. Also report `pnpm typecheck` is the gate the harness runs and that it passed.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: do not modify any test, src, ADR, or priority file — the harness is read-only over them (it RUNS suites, it does not change them).

WRITE-SCOPE: `scripts/**` only. Do not run git.
